data.name: App_AlreadyRunning
data.value: Google Photos Local Uploader is already running.

data.name: App_UnexpectedError
Settings_AuthIntro
data.value: Each user creates their own OAuth "Desktop app" client in Google Cloud (Google does not allow this creation to be automated). The wizard below guides you step by step and imports the downloaded file — otherwise, enter the Client ID and Client Secret manually (guide: docs/google-cloud-setup.md). You will never be asked for your Google password: the connection happens in your browser. The refresh token and the client secret are kept in Windows Credential Manager, never in clear text.

data.name: Settings_MaxFileSizeLabel
data.value: Max size per photo (MB, Google limit: 200):

data.name: Settings_FormatsIntro
data.value: Comma-separated extensions, without the dot. Remove an extension to exclude a format.

data.name: Settings_LocalDataIntro
data.value: The application never deletes your local photos or your Google Photos media. The deletion below only concerns the inventory, the logs, the settings and the secrets stored by the application.

data.name: Disclaimer_Duplicates
data.value: Google Photos does not allow this application to check your entire library. Duplicate detection is guaranteed only for files already indexed locally or uploaded by this application.

data.name: Status_Authorizing
data.value: Authorization in progress in your browser...

data.name: Msg_MissingCreds_Text
data.value:
The Client ID is missing or invalid (it must end with "{0}"), or the Client Secret is missing.
Do you want to open the step-by-step setup wizard?

data.name: Status_CredsSaved
data.value: OAuth credentials saved. Click "Connect my Google account".

data.name: Log_CredsSavedWizard
data.value: OAuth credentials saved via the setup wizard.

data.name: Msg_Disconnect_Text
data.value: Disconnect the Google account? The refresh token will be revoked and removed from Windows Credential Manager.

data.name: Msg_RootMissing_Text
data.value: The root folder does not exist or is not accessible.

data.name: Status_ScanDone
data.value: Scan complete: {0} images seen, {1} new, {2} duplicates, {3} incompatible.

data.name: Status_ScanCancelled
data.value: Scan interrupted. Run it again to complete the inventory.

data.name: Status_ResetFailed
data.value: {0} failed file(s) returned to the queue.

data.name: Msg_DeleteData_Text
data.value:
Delete all the application's local data?
This erases: the SQLite inventory, the logs, the settings and the secrets in Windows Credential Manager.
Your local photos and your Google Photos media are NOT affected. The application will then close.

data.name: Msg_DeletePartial_Text
Status_AuthLost
data.value: Google session expired: reconnect your account then restart the upload.

data.name: Dialog_ChooseFolder_Title
data.value: Choose the root folder containing your photos

data.name: Wizard_OpenLink
data.value: Open this step in the browser

data.name: Wizard_ImportedStatus
data.value: File imported: Client ID and Client Secret loaded. You can click "Finish".

data.name: Wizard_Validation_ClientId
data.value: The Client ID is empty or invalid: it must end with "{0}". Import the JSON file or copy the value from the console.

data.name: Wizard_Validation_ClientSecret
data.value: The Client Secret is empty or invalid. Import the downloaded JSON file, or paste the value shown when the client was created (it usually starts with "GOCSPX-").

data.name: Wizard_Step1_Body
data.value:
Google requires every application accessing Google Photos to use its own "OAuth client", created in your Google Cloud console (free, one time, about 15 minutes).
• No Google password will ever be entered in this application: the connection happens in your browser. • Google does not allow this creation to be fully automated (no public API, neither gcloud nor Terraform can create a "Desktop app" client): this wizard opens the right pages and tells you what to click. • At the end, you will import the downloaded JSON file — or paste the Client ID and the Client Secret.
Tip: keep this window open next to your browser.

data.name: Wizard_Step2_Body
data.value: 1. Click the button below to open the project creation page. 2. Sign in with your Google account if the console asks you to. 3. Project name: for example "Photos Uploader" (the name is free). 4. Click "Create", then wait for the creation notification. 5. Check, in the banner at the top of the console, that this new project is selected.

data.name: Wizard_Step3_Body
data.value:
1. Open the "Photos Library API" page with the button below. 2. Check that your project is selected at the top of the page. 3. Click "Enable".
If the button shows "Manage" instead of "Enable", the API is already active: move on to the next step.

data.name: Wizard_Step4_Body
data.value:
1. Open the OAuth consent screen with the button below. 2. User type: choose "External", then "Create". 3. Fill in the application name (e.g. "Photos Uploader") and your email address (user support and developer contact); leave the rest empty and save to the end. 4. In the "Test users" (or "Audience") section, click "Add users" and add your own Google address.
Important: as long as the Google Cloud app stays in "Testing" mode, the connection expires every 7 days (you simply reconnect). You can later click "Publish app" to lift this limit — see docs/google-cloud-setup.md.

data.name: Wizard_Step5_Body
data.value:
1. Open the credentials creation page with the button below. 2. Application type: choose "Desktop app". 3. Name: for example "Photos Uploader Desktop", then click "Create". 4. A window shows your Client ID and Client Secret: click "Download JSON" (recommended), or carefully copy both values.
The downloaded file is named "client_secret_….json" and is usually in your Downloads folder.

data.name: Wizard_Step6_Body
data.value:
Import the JSON file downloaded in the previous step (recommended), or paste the values manually.
The Client Secret will be stored in Windows Credential Manager, never in clear text on disk.

data.name: Auth_ClientCredsRequired
data.value: OAuth Client ID and Client Secret are required. See the Google Cloud setup guide.

data.name: Auth_Timeout
data.value: Authorization timed out (5 minutes). Try again.

data.name: Auth_ExchangeRefused
data.value: OAuth code exchange refused by Google ({0}).

data.name: Auth_NoRefreshToken
data.value: Google did not provide a refresh token. Try connecting again.

data.name: Auth_ScopesNotGranted
data.value: Access to Google Photos was not granted. On the Google consent screen, check all the requested permissions then try connecting again.

data.name: Auth_NoFreePort
data.value: Unable to open a local port to receive Google's response. Try again.

data.name: Auth_ClientSecretNotFound
data.value: OAuth client secret not found for this Client ID (it may have been changed). Reconnect your account.

data.name: Auth_SessionExpired
data.value: The Google session has expired or was revoked. Reconnect your account.

data.name: Log_Auth_OpeningBrowser
data.value: Opening the browser for Google authorization...

data.name: Log_Auth_ConfirmPageFailed
data.value: The confirmation page could not be shown in the browser (no impact).

data.name: Log_Auth_Disconnected
data.value: Google account disconnected (refresh token revoked and removed).

data.name: Log_Auth_RevokeFailed
data.value: Remote token revocation failed (offline?) — the local token will be removed anyway.

data.name: Auth_Html_SuccessBody
data.value: You can close this window and return to Google Photos Local Uploader.

data.name: Auth_Html_FailureBody
data.value: Return to the application and try again.

data.name: Compat_TooLarge
data.value: File too large ({0} MB, limit {1} MB)

data.name: Api_HttpTimeoutUpload
data.value: HTTP request timed out during byte upload. Automatic retry.

data.name: Api_HttpTimeoutBatch
data.value: HTTP request timed out during batchCreate. Automatic retry.

data.name: Api_EmptyUploadToken
data.value: Empty upload token returned by Google.

data.name: Api_StatusOkNoMedia
data.value: Status OK but no mediaItem returned.

data.name: Api_NoStatusNoMedia
data.value: batchCreate response without status or mediaItem.

data.name: Api_403_Quota
data.value: Google Photos quota or rate limit reached: {0}

data.name: Api_429
data.value: Google Photos request limit reached (429). Automatic retry.

data.name: Api_5xx
data.value: Google Photos server error ({0}). Automatic retry.

data.name: Log_Scan_Done
data.value: Scan complete: {0} files seen, {1} new, {2} modified, {3} unchanged, {4} local duplicates, {5} incompatible, {6} errors, {7} missing.

data.name: Scan_Reason_RemoteDuplicate
data.value: Identical content already uploaded by this application: {0}

data.name: Log_Upload_Recovered
data.value: Recovery after interruption: {0} file(s) returned to the queue.

data.name: Log_Upload_Paused
data.value: Upload paused. The current file finishes before the effective stop.

data.name: Log_Upload_Stopped
data.value: Upload stopped. Files in progress will resume on next start.

data.name: Upload_FileMissing
data.value: File not found (moved or deleted since the scan).

data.name: Log_Upload_TokenReused
data.value: Upload token reused for {0} (bytes already sent).

data.name: Log_Upload_TokenExpiredRefresh
data.value: Access token expired during upload of {0}, refreshing...

data.name: Upload_NoBatchResult
data.value: No batchCreate result for this file.

data.name: Upload_TooManyNetworkErrors
data.value: Too many consecutive network errors. Check your Internet connection then restart the upload.

data.name: Config_InvalidJson
data.value: This file is not valid JSON.

data.name: Config_WebClientType
data.value: This file corresponds to a "Web application" OAuth client. Create a "Desktop app" client, then download its JSON.

data.name: Config_UnexpectedFormat
data.value: Unexpected format: this file does not look like a client_secret_….json downloaded from Google Cloud Console.

data.name: Config_MissingClientId
data.value: The file's client_id field is missing or invalid.

data.name: Config_MissingClientSecret
data.value: The file's client_secret field is missing or invalid.

data.name: Cred_SecretTooLong
data.value: Secret too long for the Windows Credential Manager.

data.name: Cred_WriteFailed
data.value: Unable to write to the Windows Credential Manager (code {0}).
